title: lupinus lepidus var. sellulus
intro: Lupinus lepidus var. sellulus, also known as the smallflower lupine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the genus Lupinus and native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain alkaloids that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Lupinus lepidus var. sellulus include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever, as well as its antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
